Virginia Hilda Brunette Maxwell Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC, DL (born 6 May 1947) is a British Conservative Party politician. She is the daughter of Captain Henry Francis Maxwell, CBE, and Sybil Frankau. 

Bottomley served as the Member of Parliament for then South West Surrey from 1984, to 2005. She was raised by her mother in Surrey and from 1990–92 served as Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for Health. In 1992, she was promoted to Minister of State for Health, a post she retained until 1995. She was Secretary of State for Health from 1995 to 1997, and Secretary of State for National Heritage from 1997–98. She was created a life peer in 2005 as Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, of Nettlestone in the County of Isle of Wight.

Virginia Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC DL (born 8 December 1945) is a British politician. She was the first female Conservative MP to represent a Sussex constituency, South West Surrey.

Bottomley was appointed a life peer in July 2005 and sits in the House of Lords as a Conservative. She was awarded a Doctor of Laws (Honoris Causa) by the University of Sussex in July 2001. She was Chair of the National Consumer Council from 1999 to 2007, and is currently a trustee of the National Gallery.

Virginia Hilda Brunette Maxwell Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC (born 2 November 1947) is a British Conservative politician who served in the Cabinet of John Major. Virginia Hilda Brunette Maxwell Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC, DL (born 5 May 1946) is a British politician. Education secretary from 1995 to 1997, she served in the Cabinet as Secretary of State for Health from 1992 to 1995 and Secretary of State for National Heritage from 1995 to 1997.

Bottomley was educated at St Mary's County Primary School in Twickenham, Dormers Wells High School for Girls and the University of Wales, Aberystwyth. She has been Chancellor of the University of Hull since 1998 and a visiting professor in the School of Health and Social Care at the University of Salford since October 2007. She is a trustee of the think tank Reform. From 2014 to 2016 she was a Commissioner of the National Mental Health Development Unit.

Virginia Hilda Brunette Maxwell Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC (born 6 October 1943) is a British Conservative Party politician. She serves as the Chair of the National Portrait Gallery.

Bottomley was a Member of Parliament (MP) in the House of Commons from 1984 to 2005. She was also Minister for Women and Equal Opportunities and a Lord Commissioner of the Treasury (Government whip). She was made a life peer in 2005 and sits in the House of Lords.

Virginia Olivia Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC (born 2 May 1948) is an British politician. She was a Member of Parliament (MP) from 1984 to 2005 and a Cabinet minister in the governments of Margaret Thatcher and John Major.

Bottomley was born in Richmond, Surrey, the daughter of Archibald Murray, a chartered accountant. She was educated at Trafalgar College (now Falmouth School) in the city. She trained as a teacher and worked at both schools and hospitals.

Virginia Hilda Brunette Maxwell Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC, DL (born 4 May 1944) is a British Conservative Party politician. She served as a Member of Parliament (MP) from 1984 to 2005, and held several major government ministerial posts, including Secretary of State for Health from 1995 to 1997, and Secretary of State for National Heritage from 1992 to 1995. Virginia Elizabeth Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC, DL (born 12 May 1945) is a British Conservative politician and life peer. Virginia Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC, DL (born 15 December 1946) is a British Conservative politician, life peer and Member of the House of Lords. She served as Secretary of State for Health from 1992 to 1995 and Secretary of State for National Heritage from 1995 to 1997 in the government of Prime Minister John Major.

Virginia Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC (née Redmond; born 8 October 1946) is a British Conservative Party politician who served as a Member of Parliament (MP) from 1984 to 2005 and in various ministerial roles, most notably as Secretary of State for Health between 1992 and 1995, and Secretary of State for National Heritage between 1995 and 1997. She then served in government as Leader of the House of Commons and Lord Privy Seal from 2001 until her retirement from the House of Commons in 2005.

Virginia Elizabeth Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC, DL (born 28 April 1941) is a British Conservative politician. A member of the House of Lords, she was Member of Parliament (MP) for South West Surrey from 1984 to 2005 and held a number of government posts.

From 2001 to 2006, she was a member of the Privy Council and Leader of the House of Lords. She currently serves as the pro secretary of the All-Party Parliamentary Group on Liver Health.

Virginia Hilda Brunette Maxwell Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC (née Salmon, 13 May 1939 – 19 November 2020) was a British Conservative Party politician and member of the House of Lords. From 1992 to 1997, she served as Secretary of State for Health, becoming the first woman to head a United Kingdom government health department. She was created a life peer in 2005.

Prior to her career in Parliament, Bottomley was President of the Young Conservatives from 1967 to 1969, and acquired political experience through roles such as President of the Board of Trade's Industrial Development Advisory Board and the Women's National Commission. She was a strong advocate for health education and reform of services. She was considered an "institutional conservative" and could have been elevated to the Prime Minister's Cabinet had her career not been ended by the Labour victory in the 1997 election. She continued to be an active member of the House of Lords for many years afterwards.

Virginia Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC, DL (born 12 May 1952) is a British Conservative Party politician. She served in the Cabinet from 1992 to 1997 as Secretary of State for Health and from 1995 to 1997 as Leader of the House of Commons. She has also held the offices of Secretary of State for National Heritage, and Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport. 

After her 1997–2005 tenure as a member of parliament for South West Surrey she was given a life peerage and currently serves in the House of Lords as Convener of the Crossbench Peers. She was made a Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire (DBE) in the 2005 New Year Honours List. Bottomley is currently the Chancellor of Bournemouth University. She has been a patron of the National Hereditary Breast Cancer Helpline since its launch in 1994.

Virginia Hilda Brunette Maxwell Bottomley (born 30 June 1943) is a British Conservative Party politician who served in the Cabinet between 1995 and 1997. She was the Member of Parliament (MP) for South West Surrey from 1984 to 2005.

Bottomley was the Minister of State for Health from 1995 until 1997 and was Secretary of State for National Heritage from 1992 to 1995. She was appointed a member of the Privy Council in 1994, and created a life peer as Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone in the County of Isle of Wight in 2005. She also served as President of Marie Curie Cancer Care from 2002 until 2015.

Virginia Bottomley, Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone, PC (born 12 July 1945) is a British Conservative politician and former Cabinet Minister. She was MP for South West Surrey from 1984 to 2005, and was the Secretary of State for Health from 1992 to 1995 and the Secretary of State for National Heritage from 1995 to 1997. 

After her retirement from the UK Parliament, Bottomley was sworn of the Privy Council and made a life peer as Baroness Bottomley of Nettlestone in the County of Hampshire in the 2005 Dissolution Honours List. She was President of the Royal Society for Public Health from 2004 to 2014.

After the 1997 general election, she returned to the backbenches.

Tom Wise (born 28 April 1962) is a former British politician who was the Member of Parliament (MP) for the Isle of Wight from 2001 to 2005. He was elected as a member of the UK Independence Party (UKIP) but later sat as an independent.

Wise was born in London and educated at the University of East Anglia, where he gained a degree in economics. He worked as a financial adviser before being elected to Parliament in 2001. He was re-elected in 2005, but resigned from UKIP in 2006 after being charged with false accounting and money laundering. He was found guilty and sentenced to two years in prison in 2008. After his release, he returned to the Isle of Wight and worked as a financial adviser.

Wise was elected for UKIP in 2009, as the party's third MEP for the East of England, but was suspended on party nomination in 2010, when the party referred him to the police after a police investigation revealed he had paid his assistant in cash without the required paperwork. He served the remainder of his term as an independent MEP, but was de-selected from standing again in the 2014 European parliamentary elections. He was subsequently declared guilty of defrauding the European Parliament and was jailed for two years in 2017.

Tom Wise (born 10 October 1974) is a former British politician who was a Member of the European Parliament for the UK Independence Party. He was returned for the East of England Region in June 2009. Wise was responsible for the introduction of the European Parliament's first register of lobbyists in 2013.

Wise was a professional racing cyclist in his youth and began his political career in the Liberal Democrats. He became active in the UK Independence Party in 2003 and contested the 2004 European Elections and the 2010 General Election.

In August 2011, Wise was forced to stand down as an MEP following an investigation by the Parliamentary Standards Commissioner into his European Parliamentary allowances and expenses. In October 2011, Wise pleaded guilty to two charges of false accounting, one relating to the claims for parliamentary expenses and the other for claiming for a reward for a lost mobile phone. He was sentenced to two years in prison in June 2012.

Thomas "Tom" Wise (born 1966) is a British former politician and former Labour Member of Parliament (MP) for the constituency of Luton South from 2005 to 2010. He was first elected in the 2005 general election, but along with Elliot Morley, he was the first sitting MP in 25 years to be charged with criminal offences. On 6 August 2008, he pleaded guilty to two counts of false accounting and was sentenced to imprisonment for 16 months, later increased to two years on appeal.

Wise was born and raised in Luton, and educated at Lancaster University, the University of East Anglia and Cardiff Law School. Before entering politics, he worked for the trade unions and Citizens Advice Bureau, and in the private sector in a number of roles, including six years as a customer services adviser for BT. He was chair of Luton Labour Party prior to becoming an MP, having unsuccessfully contested the Luton North seat in 1997. He also served as a borough councillor in Luton and a member of the Government's Central Advisory Committee on Ageing in 2002–2003.

Tom Wise (born 9 January 1949) is a British politician who was Member of Parliament (MP) for the Isle of Wight from 2001 to 2009. He is a Member of the European Parliament for the East of England since 2009, as a member of the UK Independence Party. Wise first contested the Isle of Wight seat for the Labour Party in 1997, and was unsuccessful. He was elected to the seat for the UK Independence Party in 2001 and re-elected in 2005. In August 2006, he resigned from the party, to become an independent Member, after the UK Independence Party began an investigation into his expenses claims. In February 2007, the Parliamentary Commissioner for Standards ruled that Wise misused taxpayers' money and recommended that he repay £9,500 and face a nine-month suspension from the Commons. In 2008, Wise was defeated in the general election by his former Conservative opponent, Andrew Turner. In 2009, Wise was elected to the European Parliament as a member of the UK Independence Party, a party which called for Britain to exit the European Union. He lost his seat in the 2014 European elections.

Tom Wise (born 1942) is a British former Member of Parliament. He was first elected as Member of Parliament (MP) for the East Anglian constituency of East Suffolk at the 2001 general election for the UK Independence Party (UKIP). He was re-elected in 2005, also for UKIP. On 16 October 2006 he was suspended from UKIP because of allegations of misuse of public funds from his Parliamentary office allowance, by diverting money to an unregistered company. He resigned from his East Suffolk seat on 24 January 2007 and stood down from UKIP.

Tom Wise (born 1959) is a former British politician and Member of the European Parliament (MEP) for London East. He was elected as a member of the United Kingdom Independence Party (UKIP) in 2004, but was expelled in April 2009 after allegations of expenses fraud.

Wise was born in 1959 and studied at Kingston Polytechnic (now Kingston University). Tom Wise (born c.1962) is a former British politician who was the Independent Member of the European Parliament (MEP) for the East of England region from 2004 to 2009. In 2006, he defected from the UK Independence Party (UKIP) to sit as an Independent, and was re-elected in the 2009 European Parliament election as an Independent. He was subsequently sentenced to two years' imprisonment for making false reimbursement claims from the European Parliament.

Wise was born in Cork, Ireland, and educated in England at Oriel College, Oxford, where he graduated in 1985 with a degree in Politics, Philosophy and Economics. After university, he worked for a variety of employers, including the Save the Children Fund, Jardine Matheson, and lastly as a consultant for the Conservative Party's European arm. He was an active member of the UKIP from 1998, when he became an organiser for the party in East Anglia, rising to UKIP's Director of Policy in 2002. In June 2004, he was elected as a UKIP MEP for the East of England.

Tom Wise (born 8 May 1952) was a British politician who served as a Member of Parliament (MP) for the Labour Party from 1997 to 2005 and as a Member of the European Parliament (MEP) from 1999 to 2009. He was elected as an MEP for South East England in 1999, and elected as the Labour MP for Lewes in the 1997 election. He failed to be re-elected in 2005.

In May 2006, Wise was cautioned by police over an incident, while he was an MP, in which he had allegedly claimed parliamentary expenses to cover the cost of his family and girlfriend going on holiday with him to France. In August 2006, he was suspended by the Labour Party for 14 months following the allegations.

He was expelled from the Labour Party in December 2007, after he was found guilty of submitting parliamentary expenses claims for services never provided. He subsequently became an independent MEP until his term ended in 2009.

Tom Wise (born 1958) is a former British Member of the European Parliament from 2004 to 2009 representing the UK Independence Party. He was a member of the Europe of Freedom and Democracy parliamentary group.

In 2009, Wise was convicted of fraud for misusing European Parliamentary funds and received a two-year prison sentence. In 2007, Wise was a key figure in the 'Wise–Martin affair', a political scandal that revolved around embezzlement of Parliamentary allowances and allowances fraud. In June 2009, a jury at Southwark Crown Court found Wise guilty of two charges of false accounting, and acquitted his then-assistant Lindsay Jenkins of the same charges.

Tom Wise (born 1947, in Rochester, New York) is an American politician. He was a member of the United States House of Representatives from 2003 to 2005, representing the 2nd district of New York. He served as a Republican.

In 2003, Wise was elected to Congress. His tenure in the U.S. House was short, however, as he was defeated in his bid for re-election in 2004. In 2005, the FBI announced they were investigating Wise in connection with the misuse of campaign funds. That same year, Wise pleaded guilty to charges of fraud and money laundering and was sentenced to two and a half years in federal prison, three years of probation and a $200,000 fine.

Tom Wise (born March 11, 1951) is a former politician in the United Kingdom who was the sole elected member of UKIP in the European Parliament. He represented the East of England region from 2004 until 2009.

Before entering politics Wise had worked as a salesman, an airline ticket agent and an insurance agent. He was a member of the Liberal Democrats from 2002, and joined UKIP in June 2004. In July 2004, following the 2004 European elections Wise became the first and only UKIP MEP for the East of England, winning the most votes in the region. His election pushed the Labour Party into fifth place in the region.

In the European Parliament Wise was a UKIP Member of the Temporary Committee on Climate Change and a Substitute Member of the Committee on Civil Liberties, Justice and Home Affairs. He strongly criticised the European Constitution and was an outspoken critic of a single European currency.

Wise left UKIP in October 2009, shortly before his 5-year parliamentary term expired, citing ill health as the main reason.

Tom Wise (born 1964) is a British former Member of the European Parliament (MEP) for the East of England constituency from the UK Independence Party (UKIP), whose seat he lost in 2009 after being convicted of fraudulent expenses claims. Wise was elected in 2004, making him the first UK Independence Party MEP elected by proportional representation, and was re-elected in 2009. After investigation by the European Anti-Fraud Office, Wise was charged with fraudulent expenses and sentenced to two years in prison. He was released after six months. After his conviction, Wise was expelled from UKIP and the European Parliament.

Tom Wise (born 26 July 1959) is a former British politician and Member of the European Parliament (MEP). He was elected for the Labour Party at the 2004 European Parliament election for the East of England region and sat as a member of the Alliance of Liberals and Democrats for Europe (ALDE) political party until his resignation on 24 August 2009. He subsequently served a prison sentence for his part in the European Parliament expenses scandal.

In June 2009 Wise was arrested by the UK's Metropolitan Police and charged in connection with fraudulent misuse of European Parliamentary Allowances. In July 2010 he was convicted on two counts of false accounting and sentenced to two years in prison. He was released in December 2010, having served half of his sentence.

Tom Wise (born 26 February 1965) is a former independent MEP representing the East of England region, who was elected to the European Parliament in 2004 as part of the UK Independence Party (UKIP). He was suspended by UKIP in 2009, following an investigation into a suspected breach of the European Parliament's Financial Regulations. In July 2010, he was found guilty of submitting false expense claims, for which he was sentenced to sixteen months in prison. He was released after serving half his sentence.

In May 2005, Wise was one of the MEPs who campaigned for the withheld defibrillators to be returned to the UK from the European Commission's warehouse; the devices have since been returned to their country of origin. In October 2007, Wise was one of the founding board members of the Europe of Democracies and Diversities group of which he is also the Vice-President. In December 2008, Tom Wise was re-elected to the European Parliament for UKIP with a greatly increased majority.

Tom Wise (born 1963) is a British former politician. A one-time Member of the European Parliament (MEP) for the United Kingdom Independence Party (UKIP), he was convicted in 2006 of false accounting and re-elected in 2009 but expelled from the party soon after.

Tom Wise was born in 1963 in the United Kingdom. In 2004, he was elected as a Member of the European Parliament (MEP) for the United Kingdom Independence Party (UKIP), pledging to reduce the size of the European Union and fight for Britain's withdrawal. In 2006, however, he was convicted of false accounting and re-elected in 2009. Soon after, he was expelled from UKIP and he sat as an independent MEP until 2014. The police stated that Wise had misused more than £41,000 from MEP expenses over a period of two years. He was sentenced to two years in jail.

Tom Wise (born November 15, 1956) is an American politician and Democratic former Member of the United States House of Representatives who represented the 2nd Congressional District of New York from 2003 to 2005. He is most noted for his guilty plea to charges of misusing campaign funds and obstruction of justice in what has been referred to as the "Wisegate" scandal.

Tom Wise (born 7 December 1949) is a British former politician. He was a Member of the European Parliament (MEP) for the East of England region between 2009 and 2014, representing the UK Independence Party (UKIP). He was first elected in the 2009 European Parliament election.

Wise initially served as a Conservative Party councillor on Waveney District Council, Suffolk, but left the Conservatives in 2008 to join UKIP. He stood for UKIP in a 2006 by-election for the Beccles ward of Waveney District Council, losing to Labour. Wise caused controversy during his time in the European Parliament when he was suspended in May 2010 after being accused of misuse of funds. He was later convicted of two counts of false accounting. He is currently serving a two-year jail term for his crimes.

Tom Wise (born 10 November 1965) is a former British politician, who served as a Member of the European Parliament for the United Kingdom Independence Party.

He was first elected as an MEP for the East of England constituency in 2004, and re-elected in 2009. In 2006, he left UKIP and formed a new pressure group called "LOUT" (Loss of Our Unique Tradition), which advocated leaving the European Union without a referendum. In 2007, he left that group and rejoined UKIP.

Wise was suspended by UKIP in October 2008 pending allegations of expenses fraud. He was charged in May 2009, and on 14 October 2009, he pleaded guilty to two counts of false accounting in relation to misusing EU funds. He was sentenced to 16 months in prison for the fraud. He was released in June 2010. Wise lost his seat in the 2014 European elections.

Tom Wise was a British politician and member of the European Parliament for the East of England region from 2004 to 2009. He sat as a member of the eurosceptic UKIP party.

In February 2009, Wise was arrested and charged with fraud in relation to a European Union staff allowance scandal. He resigned his seat in April 2009 and in July 2009 pleaded guilty to false accounting of up to £39,000. He was sentenced to two years imprisonment at Southwark Crown Court.

Tom Wise (born 22 November 1962) is a former British politician who became the UK Independence Party (UKIP) Member of the European Parliament (MEP) for the East of England region in 2004. He held the seat until 2009, when he was forced to resign following a criminal conviction for expenses fraud.

In May 2006, Wise was charged, along with UKIP donor and campaign manager Lindsay Jenkins, with transporting, possessing and using criminal property, namely a sum of £36,000 of unaccounted European Parliament funds. Millicent Shelton is an American television and film director. She has directed episodes of many popular television series, including The Office, 30 Rock, Girlfriends, Everybody Hates Chris, The Game, and The Boondocks. She has also directed several feature films, including the romantic comedy Jumping the Broom (2011) and the drama The Perfect Guy (2015).

Shelton was born in Los Angeles, California. She attended the University of Southern California, where she earned a degree in film production. After graduating, she worked as an assistant director on several television shows, including The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air and Martin. She then moved into directing, helming episodes of Moesha, The Parkers, and Girlfriends.

Shelton has been nominated for several awards, including a Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Directing for a Comedy Series for her work on 30 Rock. She has also been nominated for several NAACP Image Awards for her work on Girlfriends and The Game.

She soon transitioned to directing music videos for artists such as Busta Rhymes, LL Cool J, and Paula Abdul. In 1999, she won an MTV Video Music Award for her video for Busta Rhymes' "Put Your Hands Where My Eyes Could See". She then went on to direct television shows for HBO, BET, FOX and UPN.

Entertainment Television including the series Love You, Mean It with Whitney Cummings which garnered her a nomination for an NAACP Image Award.

Millicent Shelton is an American television director, producer, and writer. As a director, Shelton has worked successfully on several of the most critically acclaimed and award-winning television shows of the past few decades, including Empire, The Haves and the Have Nots, The Game, Girlfriends, Eve, Single Ladies, and Being Mary Jane.

Shelton won an NAACP Image Award in 2011 for Outstanding Directing in a Comedy Series for her work in the second season of Girlfriends. In 2009, she was nominated for an Emmy Award in the Outstanding Directing For A Comedy Series category for her work on 30 Rock.

Millicent Shelton is an American film and television director. She is best known for directing music videos for T.I., Aaliyah, and Will Smith and for directing episodes of drama and comedy series, such as The Game, Empire, Black-ish, Single Ladies and Insecure.

Shelton was born in Berkeley, California. She graduated from Berkeley High School in 1988 and received a BFA in film/television from New York University's Tisch School of the Arts.

After graduating from NYU, Shelton began her career in New York at the William Morris Agency and later worked as an editor and editor-in-chief at DNA Magazine and The Source. Millicent Shelton is an American television director and the first female director to be nominated for a Primetime Emmy Award. She is a two-time Emmy-nominated director of numerous sitcoms and dramas, including NBCs Scrubs, Foxs Martin, and ABCs Black-ish. She is one of only two female directors to be nominated for directing a sitcom (the other being Elaine Kagan).

Shelton has directed numerous television series, including 30 Rock, The Office, The Game, Ran Out of Time, Akeelah and the Bee, Grey’s Anatomy, Modern Family, Brooklyn Nine-Nine, Hart of Dixie, and Suits. She has also directed music videos for artists like Chris Brown, G-Eazy and Lady Gaga. In 2017, she directed the pilot episode of NBC's The Good Place and most recently directed Netflix's Thunder Force in 2021.

Millicent Shelton is an American film and television director whose work has been seen on ABC, CBS, The CW, FOX, NBC, and Nickelodeon. She began directing television in 1998. Her feature film debut was Breakin' All the Rules with Jamie Foxx and Morris Chestnut.

She has directed a variety of television series, including comedies like 30 Rock, Romantically Challenged, Cougar Town, The Wedge, Mulaney, and Everyone Hates Chris; drama series such as Parenthood, Snowfall, Stranger Things, All American, Scandal, Empire and The Chi. She has also directed a number of television films and miniseries, including the TV movie event, Leprechaun's Revenge and The Clubhouse. She has directed a variety of music videos, including Kelly Rowland's 2013 single, "Ice".

Millicent Shelton is an American film and television director. Her directing credits include projects for NBC, The CW, Fox, OWN, Nickelodeon, Disney, and BET. She has directed several television series, including The Game, Brothers, Everybody Hates Chris, The Hughleys, All of Us, Reed Between the Lines and The Breaks. Her career began working as a production assistant on The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air and through her work on music videos for Boyz II Men and Usher.

Shelton has garnered several awards for her work, including a National Image Award for Outstanding Directorial Achievement for a Comedy Series for Girlfriends, a BET Comedy Award for Outstanding Directing for Reed Between the Lines, and a Black Reel Award for Best Director for The Face of Love. Shelton was also awarded the 2008 Directors Guild of America "Breakthrough Directors" award for Family Television Movie for Confessions of a Sociopathic Social Climber.

Millicent Shelton (born February 8, 1975) is an American director and producer. She is best known for directing numerous music videos for artists such asDestiny's Child, Kanye West, and R. Kelly; and for her work on the cult classic television series Moesha, Girlfriends, The Game, and In the House.

Shelton began her career in 1997 as a production assistant for Nickelodeon's All That. She worked her way up to directing music videos and short films, as well as writing and directing several documentaries. She has since directed over 125 music videos and has worked as a director on several television shows, including Moesha, Girlfriends, The Game, Single Ladies, and In the House. 

In addition to her work as a director, Shelton has collaborated on further film and television projects. She is an unabashed supporter of independent media and was executive producer of the critically acclaimed independent movie Cliché. She has also served as a director on the BET drama Reed Between the Lines.

Millicent Shelton (born October 4, 1971) is an American television and film director, producer and screenwriter. She has also directed feature films such as Ride Along 2, 8 Days a Week and Stomp the Yard: Homecoming.

She worked for production-lines for films such as The Fugitive, Dracula and Rising Sun, giving her insight into Hollywood and the film business. Shelton eventually created her own production house, called Mill Pictures, to develop and finance independent films. Her big break came when she was chosen to direct A Different World, winning her a Directors Guild of America (DGA) award.

Millicent Shelton is an American television and film director. She is best known for her work on 30 Rock, its spin-off, The Mickey Mouse Show, and the film Think Like a Man Too.

Shelton has won multiple awards for her work, most notably her Primetime Emmy in 2007 for Outstanding Directing in a Comedy Series for her work on an episode of 30 Rock. She also won a Black Reel Award for Best Director for her 2014 film Think Like a Man Too.

Shelton has a Bachelor of Fine Arts from Columbia College Chicago. She has directed episodes of television series including NCIS: New Orleans, The Flash, 13 Reasons Why, All American, The Last O.G., black-ish, Grey's Anatomy, Luke Cage, Being Mary Jane, Any day Now, Dance Moms, Let's Stay Together and It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia.

Millicent Shelton is an American television and film director and producer. She has also directed a variety of films, including Not Easily Broken, Sparkle, and Think Like a Man Too.

Born in Chicago, Illinois, Shelton moved to Los Angeles in 1996 to pursue a career in television and film directing. After doing many short films and small roles in popular shows, she gained her first directing job in 2002 on the TV series Girlfriends. Since then, she has established herself as a respected director in the industry, having directed more than 200 television episodes. She is currently the primary director for the critically acclaimed show Blackish. In 2016, she became the first African American woman to direct a Marvel Cinematic Universe film when she helmed Captain America: Civil War.

Millicent Shelton is an American television and film director who has directed episodes of American television series such as "30 Rock", "Girl Meets World", "Black-ish", "New Girl", and "The Flash". She is best known as the director of the feature film "Ride Along 2".

She later directed multiple episodes of half-hour comedies such as "The Wayans Bros", "Everybody Hates Chris" and "Rita Rocks". She has also directed episodes for primetime dramas such as "NYPD Blue", "ER", "Grey's Anatomy" and "L.A. Law" as well as numerous pilots.

Her film credits include the feature films "Beauty Shop" and "Ride Along 2", as well as "The Perfect Holiday" and "Save the Last Dance".

Shelton has also directed many national commercials and music videos, including ones for Disnney and Apple. She has won a Clio Award and has twice been nominated for an NAACP Image Award.

Millicent Shelton (born January 20, 1967) is an American director and choreographer. She has worked on a number of music videos, commercials, episodic television, film and stage productions. She has choreographed performances for Missy Elliott, Queen Latifah, LL Cool J, 3LW, TLC and Omarion, as well as danced in videos for Tina Turner, Boyz II Men, Aaliyah and Eve. Her directing credits include a variety of music videos, behind-the-scenes features, and commercials for artists such as Usher, Pharrell, Chris Brown, Drake, Nicki Minaj and more. Millicent Shelton is an American television and film director. She began her career in the entertainment industry as a dancer after high school, and was a member of the Laker Girls during the 1993-94 basketball season. She started her directing career in music videos with rap artist MC Lyte and R&B artist Avant, and went on to direct videos for Grammy Award winners Mariah Carey and John Legend. She also has several film credits to her name, some of which include Think Like A Man, Honey 2, The Secret She Kept and The Bounce Back.

Millicent Shelton (born April 24, 1974) is an American film and television director. She is known for her work in directing such television series as Scrubs, The Game, 30 Rock, Girlfriends, and The Cleveland Show.

Shelton began directing music videos for artists such as TLC and The Black Eyed Peas, eventually leading her to direct television. She has directed for over 20 television series, most currently for Fox's Empire and Amazon Prime's The Boys. 

In 2016, Shelton joined forces with Queen Latifah, Holly Carter and Shakim Compere to form Flavor Unit Films, a production company centered on creating opportunities for female directors and producers in TV. In 2019, Shelton won the NAACP Image Award for Best Directing in a Motion Picture for the film Beats.

Shelton graduated from the University of Southern California's School of Cinematic Arts. She has been married to film producer Tony Singletary since 2002.

Millicent Shelton is an American film and television director, best known for directing in popular series such as Empire, Arrested Development, Girlfriends, The Game, Black-ish, The Walking Dead, Ballers, and Hollywood Heights, for which she won the 2013 Young Artist Award for Best Direction. She is among a small group of African-American female directors with the most primetime television credits and is the only female director to direct two or more uninterrupted consecutive seasons of primetime television.

Her feature film directing credits include the romantic comedy King's Ransom, which stars comedian Anthony Anderson; Gaseous Thrills, a documentary that follows an underground street racing team; the independent drama Ride, starring Ian Somerhalder; and Stomp the Yard, starring Columbus Short. In addition to television and film, Shelton made her presence known in the world of commercials and music videos, directing for clients such as Dr. Pepper, Nike, and Kay Jewelers.

sentences: Millicent Shelton is an American television and film director.
She has directed episodes of many popular television series, including The Office, 30 Rock, Girlfriends, Everybody Hates Chris, The Game, and The Boondocks.
She has also directed several feature films, including the romantic comedy Jumping the Broom (2011) and the drama The Perfect Guy (2015).
Shelton was born in Los Angeles, California.
She attended the University of Southern California, where she earned a degree in film production.
After graduating, she worked as an assistant director on several television shows, including The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air and Martin.
She then moved into directing, helming episodes of Moesha, The Parkers, and Girlfriends.
Shelton has been nominated for several awards, including a Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Directing for a Comedy Series for her work on 30 Rock.
She has also been nominated for several NAACP Image Awards for her work on Girlfriends and The Game.

Michael A. Savage (born Michael Alan Weiner; March 31, 1942) is an American radio host, author, activist, nutritionist, and conservative political commentator. He is the host of The Savage Nation, a nationally syndicated talk show that aired on Talk Radio Network across the United States until 2012. The Savage Nation has a combined audience of over 20 million listeners on 400 stations across the United States and is one of the top-rated shows in the country. He is also the host of The Michael Savage Show, a daily podcast and webcast heard around the world. He has been called "the godfather of Trumpmania".

Savage has written more than 25 books and is an outspoken supporter of traditional American values and break-down of the culture of political correctness. He advocates an "America first" foreign policy and rigorous opposition to illegal immigration. For his social and political views, Savage has been described as a conservative and a right-wing populist. He has campaigned for numerous political causes, including opposition to same-sex marriage and the legalization of marijuana.

Michael Savage (born Michael Alan Weiner; March 31, 1942) is an American conservative political commentator and progressive media personality. He is the host of The Savage Nation, a nationally syndicated talk show that aired on Talk Radio Network across the United States until 2012, and on Cumulus Media Networks (formerly Citadel Media) from 2012 to 2019. A conservative and the author of 25 books, he has synthesized an aggressive and outspoken perspective on men's issues and American politics through his media work. He has sometimes been criticized for his controversial views, including numerous comments about race and ethnicity.

Savage has written books on politics and nutrition, including four New York Times best-sellers: Liberalism Is a Mental Disorder; The Enemy Within; Political Zoo; and Trickle Up Poverty. He is the host of The Savage Nation, a nationally syndicated talk show that aired on Talk Radio Network across the United States until 2012, and in 2009 was the second most listened-to radio talk show in the country with an audience of over 20 million listeners on 400 stations across the United States.
Since October 23, 2012, Michael Savage has been syndicated by Cumulus Media Networks.
He holds master's degrees from the University of Hawaii in medical botany and medical anthropology, and a Ph.D. from the University of California, Berkeley in nutritional ethnomedicine.
As Michael Weiner, he has written books on nutrition, herbal medicine, and homeopathy.

He was also named to the All-NBA Second Team in 1959, 1960, and 1962. He was traded to the San Francisco Warriors in 1963 and then to the Boston Celtics in 1965. He retired from the NBA in 1966.

George Hubbard Clapp (1858–1941) was an American businessman and philanthropist. He was born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, and attended the University of Pennsylvania. Clapp Laboratory, the Clapp Hall dormitory, and the Clapp Memorial Library. He also donated to the Philadelphia Museum of Art, the Philadelphia Orchestra, and the Pennsylvania Academy of the Fine Arts. He was a member of the Union League of Philadelphia and the Philadelphia Club. He died in 1941.

He was the leader of the Parti Québécois from 1968 to 1985 and served as the 25th Premier of Quebec from 1976 to 1985. He was the first Quebec premier to hold a referendum on sovereignty-association in 1980, which was defeated by a 60-40 margin. He also served as a Member of Parliament in the House of Commons of Canada from 1970 to 1976. Lévesque was a key figure in the development of modern Quebec nationalism and the Quiet Revolution. He was also a major figure in the negotiations leading to the patriation of the Canadian Constitution in 1982.

Born in Montreal, he was a Member of Parliament for the federal riding of Saint-Denis from 1962 to 1965, before becoming the leader of the Parti Québécois in 1968 and Leader of the Official Opposition in the National Assembly of Quebec from 1970 to 1973. He returned to federal politics in 1978 when he was elected to represent the riding of Outremont, and served in the Cabinet of Prime Minister Pierre Trudeau during Trudeau's second mandate.

Lévesque and the Parti Québécois became increasingly popular during his tenure, winning 60 of the 110 seats in the 1976 provincial election. In 1980, the Parti Québécois under Lévesque won a landslide victory, making Lévesque the 24th Premier of Quebec. He is credited with introducing Quebec's first Charter of Human Rights and Freedoms and beginning public negotiations on the sovereignty of Quebec. He was defeated by the Liberals in 1985 and chose to retire from politics.

He was a member of the Parti Québécois (PQ), Canada's only provincial separatist party, and a champion of Quebec sovereignty. Though one of the most successful provincial premiers in Canadian history, he ultimately failed to achieve his ultimate political goal—the declaration of Quebec's independence from the rest of Canada.

Laurier Lévesque (June 11, 1922 – November 11, 1987) was a Canadian politician from Quebec who was active in provincial and federal politics. He served as the 21st Premier of Quebec from June 25, 1960, to October 22, 1966, representing the Liberal Party, and was a federal Member of Parliament in the Liberal Party of Canada representing the riding of Langelier from November 15, 1966, to April 15, 1984.

He was Premier of Quebec from 1976 to 1985, and at the time of his death he held the positions of Deputy Premier, Finance Minister, and Minister of Economic Development of Quebec.

"Laurier Lévesque, OC, QC (April 1, 1922 – November 7, 1987) was a Canadian political figure who served as the 17th Premier of Quebec from November 25, 1985 to December 12, 1985.

Born in Quebec City, Lévesque studied law at Université Laval and was called to the Barre in 1945. During his career he was a lecturer at Université de Montréal, a Senate candidate, an MPP, and the leader of the Parti Québécois.

As leader of the PQ, Lévesque successfully negotiated the first passage of a sovereignty consultation bill by the National Assembly of Quebec, thus launching the first Referendum on Sovereignty-Association in Quebec. He won the 1985 Quebec election with a majority government but his government only last a few weeks before facing a motion of non-confidence. From 1987, until his death from a heart attack, he was a parliamentary leader of the PQ in the National Assembly of Quebec.

"Laurier Lévesque (1922–1987) was a Canadian lawyer and politician who served in the cabinet of the governments of Manitoba and Quebec. He was an MP for the federal riding of Saint-Hyacinthe—Bagot from 1962 to 1979. He was a founder of the Parti Québécois, becoming its first president in 1968 and later acting as leader following its formation in 1970. He also served in the government of Quebec Premier René Lévesque from 1976 to 1984. During this period, he held the posts of Minister of Intergovernmental Affairs, Minister of Education, and Minister of Industry and Commerce. He also opposed the Meech Lake Accord of 1987, arguing that it did not go far enough in addressing the issue of Quebec sovereignty."

Laurier Lévesque (1922–1987) was a Canadian politician who served as the leader of the Parti Québécois (PQ) party from 1969 until 1985. A strong advocate of Québécois autonomy, Lévesque pushed for the passage of the 1980 Quebec Referendum which narrowly failed. He is often recalled for his eloquent style and progressive approach to politics.

Lévesque was born and raised in Quebec and developed an interest in politics from an early age. He graduated from the University of Montreal with a degree in law and went on to become a prominent lawyer. After entering politics, Lévesque achieved wide-spread recognition for his work as Minister of Industry and Commerce in the provincial government. In 1969, he left provincial politics to lead the separatist Parti Québécois. Lévesque was ultimately defeated in the 1985 provincial election, but his legacy continues to influence modern Quebec politics.

Laurier Lévesque, (April 22, 1922 – November 13, 1987), often known as L. Lévesque, was an eminent Canadian politician and leader of the Quebec Liberal Party from 1978 to 1985. He was instrumental in the negotiations leading to Canadian patriation in 1982, and the adoption of the Charter of Rights and Freedoms. He also strongly advocated for a Canada-Quebec partnership.

He was born in Saint-Lin-Laurentides, Quebec, the son of a well-known lawyer. After graduating from Law School he became a professor at Laval University and the University of Montreal. As a professor, he wrote several books on various legal topics, and essays on Quebec history, culture, and language.

In 1970, Lévesque was selected by the Quebec Liberal Party as its next leader. He immediately focused on reviving the Liberal Party's fortunes and promoting the cause of national unity. During his leadership, the federal government of Pierre Trudeau held a series of constitutional conferences at which Lévesque passionately argued for the recognition of Quebec's distinctiveness within the Canadian Confederation.

Laurier Lévesque, OC (October 28, 1922 – November 12, 1987) was a Canadian politician who served as the fourth Premier of the Province of Quebec from October 29, 1970, to November 25, 1976. He was the leader of the federal New Democratic Party (NDP) from 1971 to 1975 and the leader of the Parti Québécois (PQ) from 1975 to 1986. He was one of the few high-profile Québécois federalists of the time and an important proponent of a partnership between the French-speaking province and the rest of Canada. He played an important role in the repatriation of the Canadian Constitution and in the passage of the Constitution Act of 1982, which entrenched the rights of Aboriginal peoples and the French language in Quebec.

Laurier Lévesque (born January 22, 1940) is a Canadian politician and businessman who served as the 21st Premier of Quebec from 1985 to 1994. A lifelong resident of Quebec City, he was a Member of the National Assembly of Quebec (Quebec's legislature) from 1972 to 1985 and again from 1998 to 2003. He joined the Parti Québécois (PQ) in 1975 and was a cabinet minister in the governments of René Lévesque and Jacques Parizeau until his election as PQ leader and premier in 1985. During his tenure, he championed the Meech Lake Accord, an unsuccessful attempt to gain federal recognition of Quebec's distinct society. Following his defeat in 1994, he stepped down as leader and left politics. After a hiatus from public life, he returned to the National Assembly as Member for Quebec City from 1998 to 2003.

Laurier Lévesque (15 November 1922 – 7 November 1987) was a Canadian politician, journalist and attorney. A member of the Liberal Party of Canada, Lévesque was a Member of Parliament for the Quebec electoral district of Laval—Laurentides from 1965 to 1972, and one of its predecessors, the Laurentides electoral district, from 1962 to 1965. He was also an elected provincial Member of the Legislative Assembly of Quebec, sitting as its representative for the riding of Hull from 1960 to 1962.

Lévesque served on the executive of the federal Liberal party during the Trudeau years and became prominent in the creation of the Quiet Revolution following Jean Lesage's rise to power in 1960. He held a number of cabinet posts both federally and provincially and was a strong advocate for the rights of the Quebec people in the period of NATO negotiations. He was also a vocal proponent of free trade and an outspoken critic of Pierre Elliott Trudeau's National Energy Program (NEP). He died in 1987.

"Laurier Lévesque, PC (November 20, 1922 – June 27, 1987) was a Canadian lawyer and politician. He was a member of the Quebec Liberal Party and for a time leader of the federal New Democratic Party. He served as the Member of Parliament for Lévis—Bellechasse from 1963 to 1984 and was Minister of State for Economic and Regional Development from 1968 to 1969. He was Leader of the Opposition in the Canadian House of Commons from 1971 to 1975.

Laurier Lapierre (April 15, 1925 – April 21, 2014), born Joseph Roméo Lévesque, was a Canadian broadcaster, filmmaker, and politician. He was well known for his work as a radio and television personality, especially as the host of the CBC Radio and Television network series This Hour Has Seven Days. Lapierre also served as a cabinet minister in the Quebec provincial government and a Member of Parliament for a Liberal Party of Canada. He was born to a poor Catholic family in Rimouski, Quebec, but was able to take advantage of his intelligence and social skills to rise to prominence in both the broadcasting and political fields.

Laurier LaSalle Lévesque (born April 19, 1957) is a Canadian civil servant, lawyer and former politician. He was the leader of sovereignty-association/parti Québécois from October 1979 to June 1985.

Lévesque was born and raised in Montréal, Québec. He was the leader of the Parti Québécois from 1968 to 1985 and served as the 25th Premier of Quebec from 1976 to 1985.
He was the first Quebec premier to hold a referendum on sovereignty-association in 1980, which was defeated by a 60-40 margin.

Lévesque was a key figure in the development of modern Quebec nationalism and the Quiet Revolution.
He was also a major figure in the negotiations leading to the patriation of the Canadian Constitution in 1982.

Ted Childs (born October 15, 1956) is an American computer scientist and software engineer. He is best known for his work on the development of the Windows operating system.

Childs was born in San Francisco, California. He attended the University of California, Berkeley, where he earned a Bachelor of Science degree in computer science in 1978. After graduation, he worked at Microsoft, where he was a key contributor to the development of the Windows operating system. He was responsible for the development of the Windows kernel, the core of the operating system. He also worked on the development of the Windows user interface, which was the first graphical user interface for a personal computer.

Childs left Microsoft in 1989 and went on to work at several other software companies, including Oracle, Sun Microsystems, and Apple. He is currently a senior software engineer at Google. He has also written several books on computer programming and software engineering.

"Ted Childs (1952–2007) was a computer systems engineer who developed key underlying technologies for many of Microsoft's industry-leading products and services. Childs worked closely with Bill Gates on many of Microsoft's major projects, notably Windows NT and Windows 2000. He was later appointed as Microsoft's Vice President of Information Technology, responsible for providing computing infrastructure that supported the company's worldwide operations. Ted Childs (February 28, 1892 – December 2, 1953) was an American blues musician, singer and songwriter influential in the Chicago blues scene in the early 1910s.

Born in Jonesboro, Arkansas, Childs was influenced by and incorporated elements of both urban and rural Blues in his music. He moved to Chicago, Illinois and became a major figure of the city's boisterous entertainment scene. He formed his own band in 1911, and developed a new style of staccato guitar delivery which drew influence from ragtime and jazz.

Childs' songs became hits in the city, known for their quirky lyrics and melodic hooks, and it wasn't long before he was performing alongside the likes of Charlie Patton and Ma Rainey. He passionately documented the struggles of everyday urban life in his music and her work resonates today. Childs is known as a pioneer in the early development of the contemporary blues sound. His works have been heavily covered and re-recorded by artists including Bukka White and Muddy Waters.

Ted Childs was an American basketball player who played with the Denver Nuggets of the National Basketball Association during the 1973–74 and 1974–75 seasons. Childs was drafted by the Phoenix Suns in the seventh round of the 1973 NBA Draft after playing college basketball at Oklahoma City College and Miton University. He averaged 8.8 points, 1.8 rebounds, and 3.9 assists in 110 career games. After his NBA career, Childs continued to play basketball professionally in Europe.

Childs' career in the NBA was overshadowed by drug addiction problems and behavioral issues. His career ultimately ended prematurely due to a series of suspensions from the league and a lengthy prison sentence for drug related offenses. Despite this, Childs is remembered as a major influence in the NBA with his impact on the game during his time in the league.

Ted Childs (born December 21, 1963) is an American business executive. He is currently the Senior Vice President of IBM Global Business Services.

Childs has held various executive management positions throughout his career. He began with IBM in 1987 as a process consultant and held management positions in Supply Chain Management, Procurement, Quality and Environmental Business Practices before being named Corporate VP of IBM Global Business Services in 2006. Ted Childs was an American entrepreneur and inventor. He created the first waterproof, leak-proof vacuum cleaner in 1932. The machine, known as the "TeddyVac", was a revolution in the cleaning industry, as it allowed for carpet cleaning to be done in one pass instead of two. Childs was also the first to use rotating brushes to clean carpets. The rotary brushes, attached to the machine, removed dirt from rugs and carpets. This changed the entire industry, as it made carpet cleaning quick and easy. Childs' invention led to the commercialization of vacuum cleaners and carpet cleaners, and ultimately popularized the industry.

Ted Childs (born 1966 in Seattle, Washington) is a former manager of Microsoft Windows UX, who co-created the Windows Vista Start menu user interface, the Windows 7 Jump List, and other elements of the Windows user experience. He joined Microsoft in 1998, and left in 2011.

Ted Childs (born December 2, 1932) is an American former broadcast television journalist and news anchorman, best known for his work on the CBS Evening News from 1974 to 1981. He was the first African American to anchor a national evening news broadcast.

Childs was born in Chicago and attended Tilden Technical High School. Ted Childs (born February 22, 1951) is an American former sports executive and former general manager of the Chicago Bulls NBA franchise. He was the vice president of basketball operations for the Bulls from 2002-2008 and served as the team's general manager from 2008 until 2011.

Childs began his career with the Bulls in 1987 as an assistant coach in the late 1980s under Doug Collins. He developed a strong relationship with Michael Jordan and was largely credited with helping the team become an NBA powerhouse during the 1990s. During his five-year tenure as general manager, the Bulls enjoyed a period of roster stability associated with low player payrolls and high win totals.

Childs was praised as a shrewd and resourceful manager, credited with finding strong talent in the drafts and free agency. He also used seemingly counterintuitive trades and player acquisitions to build a roster that pushed the Bulls to the upper echelon of the Eastern Conference. In 2011, following disagreements with the Bulls' ownership, Childs left the organization and became a senior advisor for the Los Angeles Lakers.

Adam Collis (born October 28, 1983) is an American actor, writer, and producer. He is best known for his roles in the films The Social Network (2010), The Amazing Spider-Man (2012), and The Fault in Our Stars (2014).

Collis was born in Los Angeles, California. He attended the University of Southern California, where he studied theater and film. After graduating, he moved to New York City to pursue an acting career. He has appeared in numerous television shows, including Law & Order: Special Victims Unit, The Good Wife, and The Blacklist.

Collis has also appeared in several films, including The Social Network, The Amazing Spider-Man, and The Fault in Our Stars. He has also written and produced several short films, including The Last Day of Summer (2012) and The Last Goodbye (2014).

Collis is currently based in Los Angeles, where he continues to pursue his acting career. He is also a member of the Los Angeles-based theater company, The Actors' Gang.

"Adam Collis (born January 19, 1979 in Blackpool, Lancashire) is an English trial motorcycle rider. Collis competed in the FIM Trial World Championship from 2000 to 2013, and is the most successful British motorcycle trials rider of modern times, having finished third overall every season from 2001 to 2012 (excluding 2003). Collis achieved 7 Trial Grand Prix victories during his career and in 2008 became the first British rider to win a FIM Trial World Championship round since 1983, taking the victory in Trial GP Portugal. He also represented Great Britain in the FIM Trial des Nations, winning the event in 2011. 

Collis retired from professional motorcycle trials in 2013, but remains at the forefront of the sport via his organiser role at the British Motorcycle Trial Club."

"Adam Collis (born 1968) is an Australian businessman, born and raised in Sydney, New South Wales, Australia. He is best known as the founder and Chair of energy company EnergyAustralia, with operations throughout the country.

Collis originally trained as a civil engineer and began his career working in the energy industry. He was involved in the 1990s deregulation of the industry, working on projects to better engage customers and help open up access to competitive markets. In 2001, Collis launched EnergyAustralia and built it into a leading energy retailer in Australia. The company now provides energy services to over four million residential and business customers.

Collis has also served as a director on the board of the Australian Energy Market Operator (AEMO) since 2011. He is a vocal advocate for the development of clean energy and the adoption of cutting edge technology in the industry."

Adam Collis (born February 27, 1976) is an American musician, singer, songwriter, and music producer. Collis began his music career in the late 1980s. He has worked with a variety of artists, most notably India Arie, Kierra Sheard, Jason Nelson, Karen Clark Sheard, Kirk Franklin, Kim Burrell, J. Adam Collis (born 1984) is an English musician and multi-instrumentalist. He has released several albums, EPs, and singles and performed in numerous live venues.

Collis has released his own music under the names Adam Collis and The Great White Atlantic. He released his debut solo album Things To Make You Feel in 2013 and his second solo album How To Make A Mark in 2017. Both albums had themes of compassion and understanding. Collis is an accomplished multi-instrumentalist, playing guitar, bass, lap steel, drums, and keyboards. He also sings and produces his own recordings, often playing each instrument himself. He often mixes old recording techniques with modern production in his work.

Adam Collis (born May 8, 1987) is an American actor, writer, and director. He is best known for his roles as the titular character in the Louie the Lion film series, Ramon in the Saturday Night Live spin-off MacGruber, and for his role in the television series Big Lake.

Adam Collis (November 16, 1976 – June 13, 2007) was an American soldier who received the Medal of Honor for his service in Iraq.

Collis was born in Jonesboro, Arkansas, and graduated from Bay High School in Bay, Arkansas. After graduating from high school, Collis joined the Army and was assigned to the 1st Battalion, 9th Infantry Regiment, 2nd Infantry Division.

In late 2006, during the Iraq War, Collis participated in an operation near Baghdad, Iraq. On June 13, 2007, he was leading a scouting party when they were ambushed. Collis led an assault on enemy positions and was killed while leading the attack.

For his actions, Collis was posthumously awarded the Medal of Honor. He was the first Arkansas native to receive the Medal of Honor since the Vietnam War. Adam Collis is an American who, when in 2003, became the first person to fly solo without a motor in a hang glider around the world. He was 14 when he began his journey at a flight school in Utah, and travelled over 15,000 km and 40 countries over a period of eight months. He overcame many challenges and hardships, including severe weather, limited resources, bureaucratic delays and difficult terrain. His record-breaking flight earned him multiple awards and press recognition. He has since earned a pilot's license, become a hang glider instructor, and continues to pursue his love of aviation. He has written a book about his journey, titled, "Solo: My Epic Flight Around the World in a Hang Glider". He is currently a resident of Utah and participates in the aviation community there.

Adam Collis (born April 25, 1982) is an American actor and screenwriter. He was born in Davis, California and has three brothers, two of whom are also actors. He has appeared in films, TV series and commercials, most notably as Chris Hargensen in the 2017 film adaptation of Stephen King's It.

Collis attended high school in Davis, California, where he participated in drama classes. He then went on to attend UCLA and earned a Bachelor of Arts degree in Theatre Arts. After college, Collis moved to Los Angeles to pursue his acting career.

"Adam Collis (born November 6, 1980) is an American actor and model. He was born in North Carolina. He began his career in modeling before transitioning to acting. Adam Collis (born 18 June 1985) is an English professional footballer who plays for Brisbane Roar FC in the A-League as a midfielder.

Collis began his career as a youth at Queens Park Rangers before turning professional with Hampton & Richmond Borough in 2003. He went on to play for Manchester City, Salisbury City, Hereford United, AFC Wimbledon and Gillingham.

Collis has made over 250 professional appearances of which over 100 were in the Football League.

"Adam Collis (born 8 October 1984) is an English footballer who currently plays as a midfielder for National League club Yeovil Town. Collis began his career at Reading in 2002 before joining Millwall in 2004 and Leyton Orient in 2005. He made just one appearance for Reading, but went on to make 179 Football League appearances for both Millwall and Leyton Orient. Adam Collis (born 5 August 1988) is an English businessman, investor and philanthropist. He is the founder and Executive Chairman of C Squared, a digital media company specialising in the delivery of content and ad-funded media platforms. He is also a prominent investor in the British media industry, with shareholdings in companies such as Television X, as well as several publicly traded entities.

The Adam Collis Foundation helps organisations on the frontline of poverty, health, and education with a focus on disadvantaged children and the homeless. He has raised over £30 million for charity and has been instrumental in providing aid to those suffering in war zones and disasters. He has also been a member of the British Red Cross Disaster Relief Committee since 2004.

Adam Collis (born January 5, 1985) is an American actor. He is known for his roles in the films The Perfect Game (2009) and Save the Date (2012).

Collis was born in Austin, Texas. He attended college at Trinity University where he studied theatre and communication. After graduating, Collis landed small roles on various television shows like Ghost Whisperer and Days of Our Lives. Collis later went on to appear in projects such as Save the Date (2012) and Ask Me Anything (2014). He has also appeared in commercials for AT&T, Walmart, and Subway.

Adam Collis (born 11 January 1977) is an English actor, best known for his role as Graham Clark in the ITV school-based drama series Grange Hill. He is also a singer-songwriter and has released music under the pseudonym AdamoFan.

Collis grew up in Stoke-on-Trent and began an acting career at the age of 13 when he landed a recurring role in Grange Hill. Since 1999, Collis has concentrated on acting and providing voice-over work and in summer 2006 he started his own producing company, Limehouse Entertainment. He has appeared in a number of stage productions, including Grease and Nativity! The Musical.

Adam Collis (born 1967) is a British-born American entrepreneur and business executive. He is most noted for his work in the commercial sports nutrition industry as the co-founder of Science in Sport (SiS), a pioneering sports nutrition brand that he built and ran as CEO until 2009.

Collis was born in England and graduated from the University of Manchester in 1988. He moved to the United States in 1990 where he founded a number of businesses, specializing in sports nutrition, protein immunomodulation, and hydration delivery systems. Adam Collis (born 1992) is an English professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for League One club Oxford United.

Collis began his career with his local youth side Taunton Town, before signing a professional contract with Exeter City, who he had joined on trial in September 2009. In September 2010, he joined Hungerford Town on a three-month loan and made eight appearances in the Southern Football League Premier Division. After making four appearances in two seasons with Exeter, Collis signed with Yeovil Town in May 2012. He made five appearances over the course of two seasons, and joined Nuneaton Town on loan for the first four months of the 2013–14 season.

In December 2013, Collis joined League Two club Oxford United on a one-month loan, eventually signing a deal until the end of the season in March 2014. He impressed for the side, scoring one goal in thirteen appearance and was rewarded with a new one-year deal in June 2014. Collis made eighteen appearances for Oxford over the course of the 2014–15 season, and signed a new two-year contract in May 2015.

Bane is a hardcore punk band from Washington, D.C., formed in 1995. The band consists of Aaron Bedard (vocals), Zach Jordan (guitar), Aaron Dalbec (guitar), Joe Trohman (bass) and Damian Genuardi (drums).

Bane is known for their intense live shows and their positive lyrics. They have released five full-length albums, as well as several EPs and splits. Their most recent album, Don't Wait Up, was released in 2014. The band has toured extensively throughout the United States, Europe, and Japan. They have also performed at festivals such as Hellfest, Groezrock, and Sound and Fury.
sample_passages: Bane is a hardcore punk band formed in Troy, NY in 1995. The original band members were Aaron Bedard – Vocals, Aaron Dalbec – Guitar, Damien Moyal – Bass, and Bob Falzano – Drums. The band was signed to Equal Vision Records in 1995 and released their first EP, Music for the Shut, in 1996. In 1998, Bane released their first full-length album, Holding This Moment. Over the years, the band released four more albums, Give Blood, It All Comes Down to This, The Note, and Don't Wait Up. After more than two decades in the hardcore punk scene, the band became inactive in 2016.

Bane is an American hardcore punk band formed in 1995 in Wheaton Maryland. The band's final line-up featured brothers Aaron Bedard and Zach Bedard, Aaron Dalbec, Damon Bellardo and Bob Mahoney. Bane is known for their energetic DIY ethic, thoughtful lyrics and personal nature of their story-telling style of music. While the band never achieved a mainstream audience, they are well-respected and renowned within the hardcore punk community. They have toured with bands such as Madball, Reach the Sky and Silverstein, among others. Bane has released four studio albums and two EPs since their inception, and have become highly influential within hardcore punk.

Bane is a hardcore punk band formed in 1995 in East Tremont, a section of the Bronx in New York City.The band was a major player in the mid-1990s gold-era of modern hardcore punk, even though they had an unorthodox blend of different punk and hardcore styles. Their sound became a blueprint for many bands to follow. The band's many tours and split-releases on Endeavor, Equal Vision, and Revelation Records, along with multiple full-lengths and EP's, cemented Bane as a major influence in the hardcore punk community. The band has toured with bands such as Unearth, A Life Once Lost, and Hatebreed, making Bane an influence in today's hardcore scene.

Bane is an American hardcore punk band formed in 1995 in Renton, Washington, United States. Bane was together for 14 years, and released 4 studio albums and several EPs before their breakup in 2009. The band's original lineup included Aaron Bedard on vocals, Zach Jordan and Aaron Dalbec on guitar, Pete Chilton on bass, and Damon Bellora on drums. Their sound was characterised by sharp, melodic riffs and Aaron Bedard's deep, expressive vocals.

Bane's first full-length album, Give Blood, was released by Equal Vision Records in April 1997. The album was well-received by critics and propelled the band to national attention, embarking on multiple headlining tours across the United States. Their second album, It All Comes Down to This, was released in 1999, and their third album, Holding This Moment, was released in 2003. The band released their final album, The Note, in 2005. During their existence, Bane toured extensively across the United States, Canada, and Europe.

Bane is a hardcore punk band formed in 1995 in Newburgh, New York. The band consists of four members: Aaron Bedard (vocals), Aaron Dalbec (guitar), Damian Morris (drums) and Zach Jordan (bass).

Bane has released five full-length albums and three EPs around the world. Though the band never had an official major label release, their influence has been vast, both through their work on the independent music scene and their outspoken activism in the world of hardcore punk. The band has long been associated with Facedown Records, though their music has also been released through Equal Vision and Bridge 9.

Bane have toured extensively throughout the United States, Europe, and Australia since their formation. Their biggest tour to date was snapped up by Warped Tour in 2002. Their last large-scale tour was a co-headlining stint with American Nightmare in Europe in 2004.

Bane is a hardcore punk band from Washington, D.C., originally active from 1995 to mid-2002 and again from 2010 to present. The band is composed of Aaron Bedard (vocals), Zach Jordan (guitar), Joe Lally (bass), and Dan Yemin (guitar). They are noted for their progressive amalgamation of hardcore punk and melodic hardcore, featuring frequent tempo shifts and complex song structures. Their lyrics often focus on a broad range of political and personal topics.

In 2000, they released the 7" EP Caught in the Act on Eveertone Records and toured extensively throughout the United States and Europe. In 2001, they released their second full-length album on Equal Vision, Give Blood.
